title: Palicourea sulphurea
intro: Palicourea sulphurea, also known as the "yellow jaguar" or "sulphur-leaved palicourea", is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Palicourea sulphurea include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the most common herbal preparations being decoctions and infusions of the leaves and stems.
